claims.text: The GPT-3 paper attributes one specific component to GPT-2: it reuses the byte-level BPE tokenizer of GPT-2, noting that this tokenizer was developed for an almost entirely English training dataset.
claims.verification_reason: The passage explicitly states GPT-3's weakness could be "due to reusing the byte-level BPE tokenizer of GPT-2 which was developed for an almost entirely English training dataset."

claims.text: Within the supplied evidence passages, no passage from the GPT-3 paper cites GPT-2 as an architectural predecessor; the only GPT-2 attribution appearing in the supplied GPT-3 evidence concerns tokenizer reuse.
claims.verification_reason: The supplied GPT-3 evidence passages only mention GPT-2 in the context of reusing the byte-level BPE tokenizer; no architectural attribution to GPT-2 appears in any supplied passage.

claims.text: The GPT-2 paper states in its Model section that it uses a Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) based architecture for its language models, and that the model largely follows the details of the OpenAI GPT model (Radford et al., 2018) with a few modifications.
claims.verification_reason: The passage from section 2.3 (Model) directly states: "We use a Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) based architecture for our LMs. The model largely follows the details of the OpenAI GPT model (Radford et al., 2018) with a few modifications."

claims.text: The GPT-2 paper also names the Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) in its Approach section as an example of a self-attention architecture that has improved the expressiveness of models computing conditional token probabilities.
claims.verification_reason: The passage from section 2 (Approach) directly states there "have been significant improvements in the expressiveness of models that can compute these conditional probabilities, such as self-attention architectures like the Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017)."

claims.text: No claim is made of a direct GPT-3 to Attention Is All You Need citation; the lineage is constructed only as GPT-3 → GPT-2 (tokenizer reuse) and GPT-2 → Attention Is All You Need (Transformer architecture attribution).
